channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Johannesburg's Financial Crisis Amid Rising Executive Salaries
item.description: Johannesburg is currently in a severe financial crisis, confirmed by Finance Minister Enoch Godongwana, who warned that the City of Johannesburg (CoJ) cannot afford the R10 billion wage agreement with...

item.title: Ghana Ends US Health Aid Negotiations Over Personal Data Concerns
item.description: Ghana don decide to stop negotiations with United States for one multi-year aid deal after dem Americans start to insist on access to personal data of Ghanaian citizens. This move show say Ghana dey p...

item.title: Zendaya and Sydney Sweeney Maintained Limited Interaction at ‘Euphoria’ Season 3 Premiere
item.description: At the Los Angeles premiere of Euphoria Season 3, Zendaya and Sydney Sweeney were reportedly seen keeping their distance, prompting speculation about potential tension between the co-stars. Sources of...

item.title: Children of the Blitz: BBC documentary captures moving testimonies of wartime child survivors
item.description: This BBC film, marking the 85th anniversary of the end of the Blitz in May 1941, provides a poignant and invaluable record of the experiences of some of the last surviving children of the Blitz. Inter...

item.title: President Zardari urges govt to ease inflation and ensure essential commodities amid regional tensions
item.description: President Asif Ali Zardari met Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif at Aiwan-e-Sadr on Monday to discuss measures for providing relief to the common man, especially in light of the ongoing tensions in the Mi...

item.title: Trump Administration Adjusts Beef Tariffs Amid Record-High Prices and Supply Challenges
item.description: The Trump administration announced plans to temporarily suspend certain tariffs on beef in response to record-high prices affecting U.S. consumers. The Wall Street Journal reported that tariffs applie...

item.title: Angat Dam water level dips below minimum operating threshold
item.description: The water level at Angat Dam in Bulacan has fallen below its minimum operating level of 180 meters, reaching 179.79 meters as of 6 a.m. yesterday, according to the Bulacan Provincial Disaster Risk Red...

item.title: Tottenham secure draw against Leeds but relegation worries continue
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur na im dey face serious wahala for Premier League as dem only manage 1-1 draw for their home match against Leeds United on Monday. Mathys Tel, di French player, do both the correct th...

item.title: Driver Fined for Causing Damage with Football Net on Vehicle
item.description: A driver in Hertfordshire was recently fined after an unusual incident involving a football goal net that ended up on their car. According to local police reports, the incident occurred when the net, ...

item.title: Accord condemns killing of party women leader’s son in Osun
item.description: The Accord Party has strongly condemned the killing of Kolade Eluyera, the son of its women leader in Irewole Local Government Area of Osun State. The party described the incident as gruesome, barbari...

item.title: Canal+ Strengthens Africa Presence with MultiChoice Acquisition
item.description: Canal+ Afrique don dey shake tings for African pay-TV market after e grab South Africa’s MultiChoice, wey be big player for anglophone Africa. This acquisition bring plus 40 million subscribers and co...

item.title: House approves articles of impeachment against VP Sara Duterte
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – Vice President Sara Duterte has become the first Philippine official to be impeached twice. On May 11, 2026, the House of Representatives approved the justice committee's report ...

item.title: Congress criticises PM Modi for urging public austerity measures amid West Asia crisis
item.description: New Delhi: Opposition parties, led by the Congress, have strongly criticised Prime Minister Narendra Modi for asking citizens to adopt austerity measures similar to those during the Covid period in vi...

item.title: Behind the Scenes: 32 Photos from the 1988 Film 'Running on Empty'
item.description: The article showcases 32 rare and memorable photographs from the set of the 1988 American drama film 'Running on Empty,' directed by Sidney Lumet and written by Naomi Foner. The film follows the Pope ...

item.title: Mandera Residents Protest Presence of Jubaland Forces After Border Clashes
item.description: Residents of Mandera County in northern Kenya are up in arms over reports of Jubaland forces from Somalia camping in their town after deadly clashes in the border area. The troops reportedly crossed i...

item.title: Probe ordered after deadly attack on MPA Ayub Gadhi’s residence leaves four dead
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has ordered an immediate inquiry after a violent attack on the residence (dera) of PML-N MPA and former provincial minister Ayub Gadhi in Toba Tek Singh resulted in four ...

item.title: Nigerian Army Establishes Internal Panel To Investigate Leaks of Yobe Attacks
item.description: The Nigerian Army don establish internal probe panel to check which soldiers dey leak details of the recent Boko Haram attacks wey happen for Yobe State. Na after SaharaReporters publish exclusive rep...

item.title: President Samia Directs Tanzania Prisons to Focus on Inmate Reintegration and Reducing Recidivism
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has instructed the Tanzania Prison Service to strengthen rehabilitation programmes that prepare inmates for successful return to society after serving their sentences. Sp...

item.title: ACT Wazalendo Questions Government Youth Loan Distribution, Calls for Transparency
item.description: Abdul Nondo, the chairman of Ngome ya Vijana Taifa of ACT Wazalendo, has raised concerns over the recent youth loan program announced by the Ministry of Youth Development. According to Nondo, out of 3...

item.title: Johannesburg Metro Police demolish illegal structures in CBD streets to tackle crime and by-law violations
item.description: On Monday 11 May 2026, the City of Johannesburg carried out demolitions of illegal structures along Small, Pritchard and Rahima Moosa streets in the inner city CBD. Johannesburg Metro Police Departmen...
